name: pymathis

metadata.name: pymathis
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 or later (GPLv3+)
metadata.summary: A library of interacting function to enable exchanges between MATHIS and python at each time steps

documentation: A library of interacting function to enable exchanges between MATHIS and python at each time steps

modules.name: pymathis.tests
documentation:
pymathis

A library of interacting function to enable exchanges between MATHIS and python at each time steps

modules.name: pymathis
documentation:
pymathis

A library of interacting function to enable exchanges between MATHIS and python at each time steps

functions.name: pymathis.pymathis.LoadDll
documentation:
This function loads and initiates the communication with MATHIS
\:param DLLPath: the is the absolute or relative path to the smartmathis dll
\:param C_FILE: this is the absolute or relative path the case (results are written in the same folder)
\:return: the opened smartmathis dll object

functions.name: pymathis.pymathis.CloseDll
documentation:
The function just closes the .out file so it can be cleaned or used within the same python code
\:param dll: the opened smartmathis dll
\:return: the .out file is properly closed

functions.name: pymathis.pymathis.give_passive_ctrl
documentation:
This function gives the value of each passive controller to mathis
\:param dll: the opened smartmathis dll
\:param valuein: Controller value
\:param tag: Controller index in the mathis passive controllers list
\:return: none

functions.name: pymathis.pymathis.get_probe_ctrl
documentation:
This function enables to fetch a probe value from mathis
\:param dll: the opened smartmathis dll
\:param tag: the Probe index in the mathis probe controllers list
\:return: the probe value in a numerical format

functions.name: pymathis.pymathis.get_probe_ID
documentation:
This function fetches the Probe's name for each index in mathis' case
\:param dll: the opened smartmathis dll
\:param tag: The probe index in the mathis probe controllers list
\:return: The Probe name in a string format

functions.name: pymathis.pymathis.get_passive_ID
documentation:
This function fetches the passiv controller's name for each position in mathis' case
\:param dll: the opened smartmathis dll
\:param tag: the controller index in the mathis passive controllers list
\:return: The controller name in a string format

functions.name: pymathis.pymathis.get_passive_number
documentation:
This function gets the total number of passive controllers in mathis case
\:param dll: the opened smartmathis dll
\:return: integer format

functions.name: pymathis.pymathis.get_probe_number
documentation:
This function gets the total number of probes in mathis case
\:param dll: the opened smartmathis dll
\:return: integer format

functions.name: pymathis.pymathis.init_solver
documentation:
This function initiates mathis dll, the .data is thus validate through this function
\:param dll: the opened smartmathis dll
\:param file: the .data and associate path
\:return: none or mathis error message if present

functions.name: pymathis.pymathis.give_weather
documentation:
This function provides MATHIS with outdoor weather conditions for the next timestep calculation
\:param dll: the opened smartmathis dll
\:param vmeteo: wind speed (m/s)
\:param wdir: wind direction ()
\:param text: air temperature (C)
\:param hr: relative humidity (%)
\:param sunrad: normal direct radiation flux (W/m2
\:param diffrad: horizontal diffuse radiation flux (W/m2)
\:param tsky: equivalent sky vault temperature (C)
\:param tground: ground temperature at a given depth (C)
\:param patm: atmospheric pressure at reference altitude (Pa)
\:return: none

functions.name: pymathis.pymathis.solve_timestep
documentation:
This function asks mathis to solve next time step up to the t+dt
\:param dll: the opened smartmathis dll
\:param tasked: current time (in second)
\:param dtasked: time step (can be different from DTETA in the .data, but must be in second)
\:return: none

functions.name: pymathis.pymathis.get_obj_value
documentation:
Function for recovering various quantities calculated by MATHIS without using probe controllers
\:param dll: the opened smartmathis dll
\:param objtype: type of MATHIS object - currently, only the 'LOC', 'BRANCH' or 'WALL' types are implemented
\:param objid: id of MATHIS object
\:param valuename: name of the asked object value.

> For objtype='LOC': 'DP' (Pa), 'RA' (vol/h), 'RHO' (kg/m3), 'T' (C), 'MH2OLIQ' (kg).
> For objtype='BRANCH':'QV' (m3/h), 'QM' (kg/s), 'RHOFLOW' (kg/m3), 'DPFLOW' (Pa), 'QV1' (m3/h), 'QV2' (m3/h)
> For objtype='WALL': 'TP1', (°C), 'TP2' (°C), 'HRP1' (%), 'HRP2' (%), 'QC1' (W), 'QC2' (W) , 'T' (C), 'HR' (%), 'YW' (kg/kg), 'YWMEAN' (kg/kg), 'XPOS' (m)

```
param tag

index to be considered for certain tabulated quantities (e.g. T, HR, YW, YWMEAN or 'XPOS' of WALL)

return

float format
```

functions.name: pymathis.pymathis.get_obj_number
documentation:
This function gets the total number of objects in mathis case
\:param dll: the opened smartmathis dll
\:param objtype: type of MATHIS object: 'LOC', 'BRANCH','BOUND','WALL','CTRL','HSRC','PERSON','SPEC' or 'CTRL'
\:return: integer format

functions.name: pymathis.pymathis.get_obj_ID
documentation:
This function gets the ID of the object type from the given index in mathis case
\:param dll: the opened smartmathis dll
\:param objtype: type of MATHIS object: 'LOC', 'BRANCH','BOUND','WALL','CTRL','HSRC','PERSON','SPEC' or 'CTRL'
\:param tag: index position of the object type in mathis
\:return: string format or error message with the total number of object type available for the Case

modules.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects
documentation: this Module creates the different Mathis objects for further assembly in the input *.data file

functions.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.readDotOutFile
documentation:
Function that fecth the warning messages in the .out file
\:param Path: path to results folder
\:param file: file name
\:return: dictionary of warnings messages

functions.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.getDefaultMathisValues
documentation:
fetch the defaults variables and values of mathis
\:return: dictionary of defaults attributes and values

functions.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.readDataFile
documentation:
function the reads the .res and .head file in the result folder.
used in ReadResults()
\:param path: path to the result folder
\:param file: file name
\:param Datatype: if it's a head format or a data format
\:param IdxG: index steps to fetch values
\:param StartTime : starting time at which the results are to be kept
\:return:

functions.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.loadExistingFile_old
documentation:
NO MORE USED, replaced by loadExistingFile(). its kept here for potential debug mode
function that enables to load a previous case, it can still present issues as all format of handwritten older datacase cannot be considered
:param file : fail name to load
:return : dictionary of all object found to further convert those in mathis objects
# External functions to handle the building object methods

functions.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.loadExistingFile
documentation:
function that enables to load a previous case, it can still present issues as all format of handwritten older datacase cannot be considered
:param file : fail name to load
:return : dictionary of all object found to further convert those in mathis objects

functions.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.fillInOutputDict
documentation:
fetch futur attributes for futur objects
\:param outputDict: output
\:param key: future object name
\:param att: attributes
\:return: outputdict updated

functions.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.convert
documentation: String conversation function, used for previous .data files

functions.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.check4Tables
documentation: function that deals with tables, used for previous .data file

functions.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.getCoord4Table
documentation: filling a matrix using brackets values inside a previous .data file, used for previous .data files

functions.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.WriteObject
documentation: function linked to the MakeInputFile function

functions.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.write2file
documentation:
This function is made to write the input data file needed for mathis. It's an internal function used in CreateObj()
\:param file: the opened object file
\:param header: the type of object concerned
\:param kwargs: list of attribute
\:return: none

functions.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.CreateFile
documentation:
This function create and open the file for the .data
\:param filename: name of the .data file
\:return: none

functions.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.CreateObj
documentation:
This function is used to create the corresponding object in the .data
\:param file: opened file
\:param Type: the type of object to create
\:param kwargs: list of attribute for the object to be created
\:return: the .\*data file is appended by the created object with its attributes

classes.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.Building

methods.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.Building.__init__
documentation:
Building Object initialization, if the file Name exists it will be loaded otherwise a new object
with default values will be created
\:param Name: Building Oject Name, it will be the Name.data file created as Mathis input

methods.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.Building.setMisc
documentation:
This function enable to change the Misc Object attributes value
\:param kwargs: any possible attribute of Misc Object
\:return: the new Misc Object with new values

methods.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.Building.setExt
documentation:
This function enable to change the Ext Object attributes value
\:param kwargs: any possible attribute of Misc Object
\:return: the new Misc Object with new values

methods.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.Building.addLoc
documentation:
This function enables to add a Loc Object with any attributes allocated related to Loc Object
\:param kwargs: any possible attribute of Loc Object
\:return: a now Loc Object, a message is raised if the Loc ID already exist, it wont be created

methods.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.Building.addBranch
documentation:
This function enables to add a Branch Object with any attributes allocated related to Branch Object
\:param kwargs: any possible attribute of Branch Object
\:return: a now Branch Object, a message is raised if the Branch ID already exist, it wont be created

methods.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.Building.addHSRC
documentation:
This function enables to add a HSRC Object with any attributes allocated related to HSRC Object
\:param kwargs: any possible attribute of HSRC Object
\:return: a now HSRC Object, a message is raised if the HSRC ID already exist, it wont be created

methods.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.Building.addPerson
documentation:

\:param kwargs: any possible attribute of Person Object

methods.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.Building.addSpecies

methods.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.Building.MakeInputFile
documentation: This function enables to build the .data file required to launch MATHIS either using the dll or using the standalone .exe application

methods.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.Building.run
documentation:
This function enables to run the mathis case either using the dll of in an external process the external
process option is used mainly for parallell computing when laucnhing a bunch of case but it requires the path
to mathis.exe
:param ExternalProcess : bool to use a check call cmd instead of the dll
:param Verbose : computation progression given in consol or not

methods.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.Building.ReadResults
documentation:
Function that fetch the results from reading the .res and .head files
\:param GiveDict: bool = True, results will be given in a dictionary format, = False results will be given as object with all possible variable as attributes
\:param Vars: List of variable that would be read only
\:param Steps: index growth to fetch data (every Steps withtin the data .res files), useful to get hours values for smaller time step's simulation
\:param StartTime : Start Time at which the results are to be kept
\:return: Results object or dictionary format
Creates the building class object that deals with all other Mathis' Objects

classes.name: pymathis.Mathis_Objects.Results
documentation: Class object to store de results and make them easy to plot
